Empires & Puzzles Eichbelborg Review: The Boastful Bodybuilder Guide

Eichbelborg brings a unique defense down mechanic and a massive ego to the battlefield. Our review explores if this bodybuilder still has the muscle for modern Empires & Puzzles or if he's just skipping leg day.
Eichbelborg Review: Pumping Iron & Deflating Defense
Eichbelborg, The Boastful Bodybuilder: Pumping Iron & Deflating Defense

Eichbelborg is a 4-star Epic Fire hero from the Starfall Circus event. Released back in 2021, he burst onto the scene with a waxed mustache, a striped leotard, and an ego big enough to fill the entire circus tent. As a "Boastful Bodybuilder," his role is simple: hit fast and humiliate the toughest enemy on the field.

While he doesn't have a costume to update his wardrobe or his stats, he remains a unique tool in the red roster due to his specific targeting mechanics. He’s the guy at the gym who grunts loudly with every rep but actually lifts some serious weight when it comes to defense reduction.

Visually, Eichbelborg is a delightful break from the endless parade of grim knights and ethereal sorcerers. He channels a classic 1920s strongman aesthetic, complete with a leopard-print sash, heavy chains, and a grin that says, "I definitely looked at myself in the mirror for 45 minutes this morning." The art perfectly captures his "Pompous Flex" persona—he's not just fighting; he's performing. It’s hard not to chuckle when you see him flexing next to literal gods and monsters.

, he sits significantly behind modern 4-star heroes, who often push past the
This is quite low for a sniper. Modern 4-star attackers typically boast 750-800+ attack.
His bulk is decent for an older hero, but he is squishy compared to the "Construct" or "Toon" 4-stars released recently.
He relies heavily on emblems and Limit Breaks to survive in the current landscape. Without them, he's likely to be knocked out before he can finish his posing routine.
The "Pompous Flex" is a fascinating mix of standard sniping and tactical debuffing.
340% damage at Fast speed is respectable, though his low attack stat holds back the raw numbers.
is the star of the show. This is a massive drop, rivaling 5-star heroes. However, the targeting is
hits the enemy with the highest base defense.
In raids, the "tank" (center hero) often has the highest defense. This means you can target
with the damage, but the defense down will reliably land on the tank, helping you bust open the center.
You cannot manually choose who gets the defense down. If a flank has higher defense than the tank, or if you're trying to snipe a wing, the defense down might land elsewhere, disjointing your combo.
. With a level 29 mana troop or a combination of costume bonus (none here) and emblems/bard bonus, he can reach 7 tiles, though 8 is his standard operational speed.
A modern 4-star like a costumed Kelile hits for similar percentages but off a much higher attack stat.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Starfall Circus family bonus is excellent, especially in 4-star tournaments where cleansers might be scarce. A 40-60% chance to
at the end of the turn is a passive lifesaver. It can shrug off a burn, a blind, or even a defense down without you lifting a finger.

is an okay Aether power, but it doesn't synergize perfectly with his role as a sniper. He wants to kill things quickly, not get hit. However, since his defense is on the lower side by modern standards, punishing enemies for hitting him adds a little extra chip damage.

Upon release, Eichbelborg was hailed as the "Wilbur replacement" for Titans for players who wanted faster animations and higher tile damage without the shared damage mechanic (which can be a double-edged sword). He was a staple in many Green Titan teams.
Power creep has been unkind to his stats. He simply doesn't hit hard enough to be a primary threat in 5-star arenas anymore. However, his
(-44% defense down) keeps him relevant. Defense down is the single most important status effect for high scores and kills.
(especially Bloody Battle where his fast speed puts pressure on). He is no longer a "must-level" for general gameplay, but for specific jobs, he is still the right tool.
Maximizes his tile damage against Titans and increases the punch of his special skill. Since his primary role is often strictly for the defense down and tile damage, Attack is king.
He remains squishy and might die to a couple of slashes from a strong boss.
Ensures he survives long enough to fire his skill at least once or twice in raids/tournaments.
Sacrifices his already mediocre attack stat, making his "snipe" feel more like a "slap."

. Eichbelborg is a utility hero; you bring him to break defense and amplify your other heroes' damage. Maximizing his attack stat helps your tile damage, which is crucial for the Titan hits where he shines most.
-44% is a top-tier percentage for a 4-star hero.
Charges quickly (8 tiles), allowing him to fire before many enemies.
Can target one enemy with damage while debuffing another (usually the tank), allowing for split pressure.
The family bonus provides free survivability against DoT and debuffs.
His base stats are very low compared to 2024-2026 heroes.
You cannot choose who gets the defense down; it is strictly math-based (highest defense).
Unlike many peers, he hasn't received a costume stat boost yet.
Dies easily to modern heavy hitters.
His best role. Fast speed + High Defense Down = High Scores. He is a great alternative to Gormek or Wilbur if you want pure defense down.
Fast speed snipers are king here. His ability to soften up the tank while hitting a threat is valuable.
Fast mana and defense down are great for clearing boss waves quickly.
This covers the "Holy Trinity" of Titan killing: Defense Down (Eichbelborg), Elemental Defense Down, and Attack Boost. Eichbelborg replaces standard Gormek here for faster mana.
Use Eichbelborg to slap the -44% defense down on the tank (who usually has the highest defense). Then follow up with C. Kelile or another fast sniper to delete the tank instantly.

If a Taunt hero is active, Eichbelborg is forced to hit them. However, since Taunt heroes usually have high defense, his debuff naturally wants to go there anyway, which can actually be a soft counter
the Taunt if you time it right.
Don't pair them. Wilbur overwrites Eichbelborg's -44% defense down with his own, and the shared damage mechanic negates Eichbelborg's sniping potential.
. A fast sniper with defense down is incredibly valuable for clearing map stages and fighting early Titans. He is much better than the standard Colen or Kelile for boss fights.
You likely have better options for 5-star wars. However, keep him for
. Do not limit break him purely for stats unless you are competitive in 4-star tournaments.
The community consistently struggles with his name, often referring to him as "Eichelborg" or just "The Strongman."
There was a significant debate upon his release about replacing Wilbur. The consensus settled on: "Wilbur for survival and consistency, Eichbelborg for speed and higher damage ceilings."
Players noted his special skill sound and animation feel like they came straight out of a cartoon, fitting the circus theme perfectly.

Eichbelborg is a hero who has aged gracefully in utility, if not in physique. His stats may be "flabby" compared to the steroids-infused heroes of today, but his technique (-44% Def Down at Fast speed) is still championship material in the right weight class. He won't carry your team alone, but he'll set up the pins for your heavy hitters to knock down.
A solid 4-star specialist. Keep him for Titans and Tournaments, but don't expect him to bench press a modern 5-star team.
